summary: PVF dispatchers still resolve sender-chosen handler strings with Call Compile; init already precompiles SRVFNC*/CLTFNC* globals, so a validated missionNamespace lookup can close the dispatch trust boundary without changing the high-level protocol.
nextAction: Patch source Chernarus dispatchers to use allowlisted missionNamespace getVariable lookup, then smoke valid server/client commands plus forged invalid command rejection.

summary: The client Tactical menu gates ICBM locally, but a forged RequestSpecial payload can reach the server ICBM branch and spawn NukeDammage without server-side commander/upgrade/funds validation.
nextAction: Implement the ICBM playbook server-side: re-derive side/team/commander, validate upgrade/module/funds/object state, debit server-side and reject malformed payloads.

summary: Side-supply mutation is a direct temp publicVariable path for west/east; DR-22 shows overspend can become a windfall and DR-44 shows the same channel is directly client-writable without a server-verifiable requester.
nextAction: Clamp negative west/east supply floors and validate temp-channel side/amount first; broader DR-44 authority needs server-owned state or a request shape with a verifiable requester anchor.

summary: ATTACK_WAVE_INIT bypasses PVF and trusts client-supplied supply/side, allowing forged discounts that can drive side-wide unit prices to zero or negative.
nextAction: Treat ATTACK_WAVE_INIT as a request, re-derive server side supply, validate requester/side, clamp modifier/duration and preserve documented JIP behavior.

summary: Construction, defense, upgrades, player buys, structure sale, gear/EASA/service, supply rewards and WASP HQ recovery repeat the same client- or payload-authoritative spend/effect pattern.
nextAction: Do not patch these as isolated UI bugs. Pick a server-ledger/request model, then migrate flows in roadmap order with dedicated smoke records.

summary: Current source Chernarus still uses random varQueu token churn and the crewless branch still exits before the normal local queue decrement; public queu broadcast reduction remains a separate UI-aware follow-up.
nextAction: Patch source Chernarus, propagate generated Vanilla, then run Arma smoke for repeated crewless/crewed buys, concurrent buyers and factory-dead cleanup. Leave public queu broadcast reduction separate.

evidenceRefs: origin/release/2026-06-feature-bundle a9219d88 Chernarus supplyMissionStarted.sqf:50-56 narrowed; Vanilla :28 broad
nextAction: For docs/source, smoke command-center delivery/no-completion near unrelated objects, then continue cooldown casing, reward/source authority and vehicle handler idempotency. Before release/master claims, port and verify the narrowed scan in both Chernarus and Vanilla on the target branch.
summary: Supply mission authority cleanup remains open. The command-center scan narrowing is present in docs/source Chernarus and maintained Vanilla, stable origin/master remains broad, and release a9219d88 carries the narrowed Chernarus scan but not Vanilla; Arma smoke and broader reward/cooldown/handler authority work remain.

docs/source Chernarus and Vanilla register HandleParatrooperMarkerCreation and ship the sender/handler pair. Stable origin/master and release a9219d88 omit the registration in both maintained missions; Arma smoke and modded drift cleanup remain.
evidenceRefs: origin/release/2026-06-feature-bundle a9219d88 Chernarus Init_PublicVariables.sqf:41 NukeIncoming only; Vanilla :39 NukeIncoming only
nextAction: Smoke paratrooper drop marker creation for docs/source. Before stable/release claims, port the client PVF registration to both Chernarus and Vanilla on the target branch, then smoke and decide divergent modded folder handling.

summary: MASH tents may still work for respawn, but MASH marker sync is not proven live: server relay code exists, the client receiver compile is commented, and no live client broadcast of the creation channel was found.
nextAction: Either revive with a server-held marker list, unique names and server-held resend/pull for JIP clients, or remove the dead relay/receiver path intentionally.

evidenceRefs: origin/release/2026-06-feature-bundle a9219d88 Chernarus Init_Client.sqf:565,589 duplicate; Vanilla :561,585 duplicate
summary: docs/source Chernarus and Vanilla now have one Skill_Init.sqf call at Client/Init/Init_Client.sqf:547 followed by WFBE_SK_FNC_Apply at :571. Stable origin/master and release a9219d88 still duplicate Skill_Init; Arma Soldier/respawn smoke remains.
nextAction: Smoke Soldier/non-Soldier AI caps, respawn skill reapply and dedicated/JIP client init for docs/source. Before stable/release claims, port the single-init shape to both Chernarus and Vanilla on the target branch.

evidenceRefs: origin/master serverFpsGUI.sqf:4 and monitorServerFPS.sqf:2 old loop shape

origin/release/2026-06-feature-bundle a9219d88 Chernarus serverFpsGUI.sqf:4 guarded and Init_Server.sqf:594-596 monitor removed/commented; Vanilla serverFpsGUI.sqf:4 and monitorServerFPS.sqf:2 old loop shape
summary: docs/source Chernarus and Vanilla FPS publishers exit on !isDedicated before looping. Stable origin/master keeps the old loop shape; release a9219d88 carries the guarded/removal shape in Chernarus but Vanilla still has the old loops; smoke remains.
nextAction: Smoke dedicated RHUD/server FPS publishing and hosted/listen no-spin behavior for docs/source. Before release-complete claims, port/verify the Chernarus shape in release Vanilla too, or explicitly scope the release as Chernarus-only.

summary: WASP marker naming still has a sleepless display-54 wait in current source Chernarus; the display-12 sibling already uses sleep 0.1. Map-marker smoke remains after a source patch.
nextAction: Patch display-54 wait throttling in source Chernarus, propagate Vanilla, then smoke map double-click/Enter/Escape marker naming behavior and timeout/no-dialog input re-enable.

summary: Dialog/title resource IDD reuse is confirmed, including EASA/Economy sharing 23000 and title resources sharing 10200; normal dialog flow mitigates some risk, but future findDisplay/debug/control lookups can hit the wrong display.
nextAction: Assign unique display/title IDs, audit findDisplay assumptions, then smoke EASA, Economy, RscOverlay and OptionsAvailable flows.

summary: Current source/Vanilla still pass [_side,_commander] into Server_AssignNewCommander while the helper assigns _side = _this, so helper-side routing receives an array instead of SIDE. Both caller and helper contain new-commander-assigned sends, but the helper route is currently blocked by the malformed destination; after fixing call shape, choose exactly one notification owner.
nextAction: Patch source Chernarus, propagate Vanilla, choose one reassignment notification path, then run hosted/dedicated/JIP commander reassignment smoke. Keep requester/role validation separate.

summary: AI commander constants/state and upgrade worker exist, but no proven live scheduler starts full autonomous commander production/logistics or sets the running flag end-to-end.
nextAction: Owner should decide revive/remove/defer before autonomous AI supply-heli/logistics work; player-run PR #1 supply heli should remain separate.

summary: Public-variable and PVF networking channels now have source-backed sender/receiver/payload mapping with JIP and authority boundaries, plus unsafe-channel candidates added to Feature-Status-Register.
evidence: Source scan found authoritative transport dispatch and direct publicVariable writes across missionNamespace and side-effect channels

No handler ownership or validation existed for a few dangerous channels (notably wfbe_supply_temp_*, ATTACK_WAVE_INIT sender/receiver assumptions, and MASH/paratrooper direct handlers), all captured with file references in docs
Direct channel table now separates confirmed-safe, risky, and likely-racy flows for future refactoring.

summary: Miksuu PR #10/#11/#12 prove supply mission start logic has regressed through exploit/UX/JIP iterations; future variants need server-owned validation and late-join tests.
nextAction: Keep supply start/reward hardening server-owned; add JIP and remote-start smoke to implementation PRs.

summary: Miksuu town AI performance work needed follow-up activation restoration and defender filtering; future optimizations should not reduce scans without preserving wake signals and diagnostics.
nextAction: Require active-town, pre-capture, remote-player attack and defender-bleed smoke checks for town-AI scan changes.

summary: Deep upstream history shows AntiStack/RequestJoin repeatedly needed semantic, variable-type, DB parsing and rollback fixes from 2022-2024.
nextAction: Require launch-join, late-join, teamswap, AntiStack-disabled, DB-unavailable and monitoring-only validation for future AntiStack changes.

summary: Deep upstream history confirms copied/generated map folders and ignored version.sqf files caused clean-repo load failures, culminating in Tasmania removal.
nextAction: Treat generated version.sqf presence, LoadoutManager output and clean-checkout mission load as release gates for map work.

summary: Old upstream branch and PR names can be misleading: oldMasterBranch is a tombstone, A3 branches are concept-only for OA, PR #3 HQ repair pricing was later reverted, and PR #9 endgame music closed unmerged.
nextAction: Classify branch family and inspect later reverts before reviving old branch or PR code.

summary: Older commits show repeated client lifecycle, UI state and marker locality regressions around init completion, MenuAction globals, nil/null checks, marker side audience and marker position caching.
nextAction: Client/UI/marker changes need lifecycle ordering, private dialog state, side-aware marker helper and JIP/moving-marker smoke checks.

summary: Construction refactors, feature removals and supply performance attempts have reverted or required cross-module cleanup; removals affected FSM/HC/buy paths and supply optimizations used wrong authority context.
nextAction: Document call contracts, grep full runtime ownership, and smoke dedicated-server/JIP contexts before construction/removal/performance work.

summary: Current packaging is Chernarus plus generated Vanilla/Takistan unless modded propagation is restored; LoadoutManager release flow depends on 7za, generated version.sqf, terrain post-copy repairs and sound filename contracts.
nextAction: Validate packaging scope, env var 7za, generated version.sqf, Takistan map ID, sound filename shape, tool logs and git diff after LoadoutManager runs.

summary: HQ repair escalation was reverted until server-owned, 75k nukes were live-test data, ICBM request/debit authority remains client-risky, and Maverick/Spike/bomb branch names are experimental rather than current truth.
nextAction: Move HQ repair/ICBM authority server-side before new economics; inspect current handler/loadout values and runtime-test ordnance before changing bombs or aircraft weapons.

summary: Town activation, HC delegation, marker blinking and cleanup all have deeper negative history: pre-capture activation restored, defender tags added, multi-HC routing remained complex, blinking defaulted off, and cleanup fixes required array/idempotency/occupancy checks.
nextAction: Keep marker blinking conservative; test AI/HC/cleanup changes for pre-capture activation, defender tagging, typed HC update-back, EH teardown, color restore, idempotency and full occupancy.

summary: DroneStrike and ReconUAV are separate support branch review lanes. feat/drone-saturation-strike head 8ca4be90 carries latest drone tuning; feat/recon-uav head 563418ea replaces old UAV scripts with server AI-flown ReconUAV/ReconUAVRecall but only includes drone history through 93b47594. Mission runtime changes are source-Chernarus-only on both branches; total branch diffs are larger because docs/superpowers plan/spec files are included. Current-Source-Status-Snapshot and the branch-only smoke pack are mandatory before merge/release wording.

origin/master..origin/feat/recon-uav: 25 files +1461/-657 total branch diff; mission runtime subset is 22 source-Chernarus files +593/-657 and 0 maintained Vanilla mission files

Record actual test evidence against the branch-only feature smoke pack before changing planned-not-run status or calling either branch release-ready.

summary: Stable origin/master remains partial/latent for AI commander automation. origin/feat/ai-commander head 4dba060e adds Chernarus-only supervisor/workers/order execution and default-on behavior, but does not propagate Vanilla or restore the missing old supplytruck.fsm logistics path. Current-Source-Status-Snapshot and the branch-only smoke pack are mandatory before merge/release wording.

Record actual test evidence against the branch-only feature smoke pack before changing planned-not-run status or calling the branch revived.

It changes Chernarus and maintained Vanilla Rsc/GUI/HUD color/font/texture surfaces and adds docs/superpowers plan/spec/mockup artifacts. It is branch evidence only until visual smoke, packed texture/font validation and branch whitespace cleanup are complete.

summary: origin/feat/buymenu-easa-qol head a66d4691 is a narrow Chernarus UI branch: unaffordable Buy Units displayed-price tint, live factory-tab queue counts, selected-unit cost display and EASA current-loadout highlight/preselect. no stats hits in Missions_Vanilla on feat/player-stats

It spans DR-22 side-supply clamp shape, DR-33a crewless factory queue cleanup, DR-2 paratrooper marker PV registration, mine/dead-object/resource-loop cleanup, patrol exit conditions, camp-bunker nil-code EH removal, kill-assist bounty type and WASP off-by-one/nil-default fixes. Treat as candidate fix evidence, not stable master truth.

summary: origin/feat/commander-positions head 560db61c adds source-Chernarus WDDM commander-buildable defense positions and modular base-wall anchors, but it is a broad 83-file branch with unrelated Valhalla/UI/AFK/HC/perf deltas and source whitespace hits. Local static validation with Tools\Validate-ZargabadMission.ps1 passed at e9294ede, but hosted/dedicated/JIP/HC runtime proof remains open.

summary: origin/feat/supply-helicopter head 262dc431 is now line-audited in the PR #1 page. Current head moves heli load/cash-run semantics to Aircraft Factory upgrades 3/4, adds load/unload timers, keeps the supply-heli runtime source-Chernarus-only, and leaves SupplyByHeli retained after completion because only SupplyAmount/SupplyFromTown are cleared.
nextAction: Smoke disabled/enabled heli, Air 2/3 gate, Air 4 cash run, timers, payout/interdiction/JIP, same-vehicle reload after completion and resolve Vanilla propagation plus non-supply branch baggage.

summary: Current supply mission code grants the completing player raw SupplyAmount, where SupplyAmount is computed from town supply value * WFBE_C_ECONOMY_SUPPLY_MISSION_MULTIPLIER * supply upgrade modifier. stringtable.xml STR_Supplies_2 still says supply truck delivery pays 4 x actual value, and WFBE_C_PLAYERS_SUPPLY_TRUCKS_DELIVERY_FUNDS_COEF = 4 is defined but not consumed in this live reward path.
nextAction: Update stringtable/help text to match live formula or update formula and copy together during supply reward authority redesign.

summary: AI commander upgrade validation treats upgrade costs as [supply,funds], matching the player upgrade menu, but the deduction path appears to subtract supply from AI commander funds and funds from side supply.
nextAction: Confirm cost tuple order and patch Server_AI_Com_Upgrade before reviving autonomous AI commander upgrade loops.

summary: The resource updater gates side supply, player paychecks and AI commander funds behind the town-supply cap guard, and income system 4 server payout applies a 1.5 multiplier not reflected by Client_GetIncome display math.
nextAction: Treat resource-loop edits as balance-sensitive and smoke actual paycheck versus UI/RHUD display for income system 4.

summary: Commander vote restart is a client-triggered RequestCommanderVote path with payload side/name, commander Set Task UI is visible but SetTask sends are commented, and HQ deploy/pack travels through generic RequestStructure without HQ-specific server requester checks.
nextAction: Include vote restart, task affordance and HQ deploy/pack authority in commander/HQ hardening smoke; hide or revive SetTask deliberately.

summary: Headless-client registration rejects owner id 0 with only a warning and no retry, while client-FPS delegation mode trusts UID/FPS payloads that later drive delegator selection. Separate Arma high-command UI HCSetGroup add path appears inert behind _hc_enabled=false.
nextAction: Add HC registration retry/reconciliation if relying on HC mode; validate sender/UID/rate before using client-FPS delegation publicly; keep high-command UI path separate from headless-client docs.

summary: Vote refresh loops use WFBE_Client_Teams_Count as an inclusive max, help menu load/unload namespace keys are mismatched, and WASP marker double-click disables all user input while running an unslept display poll.
nextAction: Run a small UI cleanup pass and smoke vote refresh, help open/close and rapid map marker double-click/dialog-close behavior.

summary: Factory purchase is still client-local with no RequestBuyUnit/RequestBuildUnit PVF, local debit after BuildUnit spawn, no explicit refund on destroyed-factory abort and no queue/refund cleanup in Server_BuildingKilled.
nextAction: Decide refund policy and server acceptance model before expanding expensive/exploit-sensitive factory purchases.

summary: HC registration appends group _hc to WFBE_HEADLESSCLIENTS_ID without visible dedupe; retry/reconciliation work should make registration idempotent before adding repeated connected-hc sends.
nextAction: Add dedupe/idempotent registration before HC retry logic and smoke repeated connected-hc publication plus disconnect cleanup.

summary: Vote menu has a likely row-color offset in GUI_VoteMenu.sqf:74-83 in addition to the inclusive team-count loops already documented.
nextAction: Fold row coloring into the vote refresh cleanup and smoke multiple candidate/vote-total states.

summary: Server_HandleSpecial upgrade-sync reads side from _args but upgrade id/level from _this, unlike surrounding RequestSpecial tag handlers that consistently consume _args.
nextAction: Normalize upgrade-sync payload parsing to one argument shape and smoke upgrade completion synchronization.

summary: Stable master has human commander order UI, replicated team state and waypoint helper scripts, but no source-proven autonomous commander scheduler that owns those primitives.
nextAction: Keep stable-master docs saying partial/order plumbing only; use feat/ai-commander branch gates before calling autonomy revived.

summary: Server commander election counts AI/no-commander votes but the winner condition selects any non-tied player candidate because _highest is always >= or <= _aiVotes. Client vote preview can show AI/no commander for the same vote distribution, creating a server/UI mismatch.
nextAction: Decide intended commander vote semantics, patch server condition and smoke player-majority, no-commander-majority, equal-vote, tie and revote cases. Keep vote-resolution semantics separate from RequestCommanderVote trigger hardening and DR-15 manual reassignment call shape.

summary: Commander reassignment UI stores team index values in rows but resolves the selected target by visible leader-name text before sending RequestNewCommander.
nextAction: When fixing DR-15, use row value/team identity instead of name text and smoke duplicate names or team changes while the dialog is open.

summary: Visible gear template list hides templates whose stored upgrade level exceeds current WFBE_UP_GEAR, so saved higher-upgrade templates can appear deleted until upgrades catch up.
nextAction: Decide whether to keep invisible filtering or show locked rows; preserve the separate _u_upgrade save-filter bug as confirmed source-unpatched.

summary: Lobby parameter WFBE_C_MODULE_WFBE_IRS does not match the runtime variable WFBE_C_MODULE_WFBE_IRSMOKE used by common init and upgrade gates.
nextAction: Choose one variable name or add an explicit alias after parameter import; smoke lobby off/on behavior and IR-smoke upgrade availability.

summary: Several parameter-surface rows do not behave like normal live lobby switches: upgrade clearance is hidden/commented but still consumed, volumetric weather is visible but forced to 0, and BIS_HC looks orphaned while headless delegation uses WFBE_C_AI_DELEGATION.
nextAction: Align visible parameters, constants and runtime consumers before documenting operator behavior or changing balance.

summary: Buy Units initializes/toggles WFBE_C_DRIVER_ENABLED_BY_DEFAULT but most crew/count/cost paths read and write lowercase wfbe_c_driver_enabled_by_default, so checkbox state, price preview and actual crew selection can drift.
nextAction: Normalize one profileNamespace key and smoke checkbox state, crew cost, AI cap and spawned crew.

summary: Empty supply trucks are deliberately kept for 24 hours by Server_HandleEmptyVehicle, so long-lived abandoned logistics vehicles are design/config behavior rather than ordinary empty-vehicle collector failure.
nextAction: Decide whether 24h supply-truck persistence is intended logistics support or should become a shorter/parameterized cleanup rule.

summary: Both current salvage payout paths call ChangePlayerfunds with a lowercase f, while the client compile exposes ChangePlayerFunds. This is a sharper broken-payout bug layered on top of the existing client-led salvage authority and loop-condition issues.
nextAction: Patch the casing in source Chernarus and maintained Vanilla, then smoke one normal engineer/salvage-vehicle payout before broader salvage authority work.

summary: Client_GetRespawnAvailable still contains commented command-center and service-point candidate code, but current respawn eligibility does not add service points as spawn nodes. Service points should be documented as support/service/EASA nodes unless owner deliberately revives respawn-at-service behavior.
nextAction: If service-point respawn is desired, revive it intentionally with UI/JIP/default-gear smoke; otherwise keep service-point work in service/EASA support docs.

summary: Server side wfbe_basearea is initialized empty, HQ deploy sends client RequestBaseArea, and server basearea FSM only prunes; no source append to server list was found in the 2026-06-04 depth scout.
next: Restore/server-own base-area seeding and smoke grouped-base defense placement/sale with grouped-base mode on/off.

summary: Supply completion message is reward-affecting: server broadcasts amount/player and the client handler grants funds plus sends RequestChangeScore for the payload player.
next: Move personal reward/score authority server-side or validate/bind completion message to server-derived records before treating the channel as cosmetic.

summary: RequestSpecial group-query trusts payload group/player/side and can move a payload player into an AI group when liveness and wfbe_uid checks pass.
next: Derive requester/player/side relationship server-side before any group move; reject stale/wrong-side/group spoof payloads.

summary: Threeway defender respawn can include zero-camp towns because total-camp helpers return 1 for empty camp arrays and GetRespawnThreeway checks equality.
next: Split real-count and safe-denominator helpers or explicitly exclude zero-camp towns in threeway respawn; smoke 0/partial/all-camp towns.

summary: AntiStack enabled mode depends on a separate A2WaspDatabase extension that is absent from the repo; existing technical audit coverage is now complemented by an explicit owner gate before public hosting.
nextAction: Owner must either confirm/install A2WaspDatabase and harden return handling/circuit breakers, or operate/default AntiStack disabled until the dependency is real.

summary: Server init launches monitorTeamToJoin with enabled AntiStack loops, but the script only computes a local weaker-side value and exits without durable output or join-policy effect.
nextAction: Future AntiStack owner should remove the launch as noise or connect the worker to an explicit output contract; do not describe it as active join balancing today.

summary: HC-created static-defense units remain fire-and-forget server-side. Existing commented update-back would send only _teams and no server branch exists; tracking needs payload/receiver design or explicit one-way acceptance.

Server_HandleSpecial.sqf mixes _args and _this in the upgrade-sync case, but _args is assigned from _this at function start, so current behavior is equivalent. Treat as cleanup debt rather than a proven broken branch.
nextAction: Standardize on _args select 2/3 during RequestSpecial cleanup; do not prioritize as a runtime bug without new evidence.

summary: The vote menu coloring path iterates vote rows but colors with [_i+1,0], making the row-offset bug concrete rather than merely likely; related help namespace mismatch and WASP input-disable wait remain UI/UX debt.
nextAction: Fix vote row index handling, align help onLoad/onUnload namespace keys and add a bounded yield/restoration path around WASP marker input disable.

summary: Community-And-Dev, Developer-History-And-Upstream-Lessons and Upstream-Miksuu-Commit-Intel overlap; a single decision ledger should track upstream PR/commit/revert rationale, branch scope, behavior classification and current action.
nextAction: Create Community-Decision-Ledger before the next large upstream cherry-pick/import pass.

summary: Maintained source Chernarus and Vanilla Takistan can create local MASH respawn objects, but shared MASH map markers are orphaned: deploy does not send WFBE_CL_MASH_MARKER_CREATED, the receiver compile is commented, and the active server relay has no maintained-mission sender. Modded eden/lingor show sender-only drift, not source/Vanilla health.
nextAction: Owner should either revive MASH markers as a server-authoritative registry with JIP replay and unique marker names, or remove/archive relay and receiver code as dead feature scaffolding.

summary: Town mortar support exists as a defense-management scaffold, but no maintained-mission town init setter for wfbe_town_mortars was found and Server_SpawnTownMortars reads the wrong local variable shape after loading _position. Keep it out of working town-defense claims.
nextAction: Revive only with explicit town anchor setup, variable-name fix and Arma smoke; otherwise archive/remove with other dead defense hooks.

summary: A focused playbook now ties DR-47 commander vote server/UI mismatch, DR-15 reassignment call shape, reassignment UI identity and requester-authority boundaries into one implementation/smoke route. Source remains unpatched.
nextAction: Owner decides vote semantics, patches vote worker/UI preview and DR-15 helper/UI identity in a scoped commander branch, propagates Vanilla, then runs the playbook smoke matrix.

summary: Independent camp capture writes the new camp sideID but sets the flag texture from the old owner; camp repair can also change camp sideID without refreshing the flag object.
nextAction: Patch flag texture to use the new side on capture and refresh flag visuals when repair changes sideID; smoke independent capture and repair.

summary: server_town_ai launches server_patrols only when wfbe_patrol_active is false, but server_patrols loops while the game is not over and clears the active flag only after the loop exits, so dead patrols can block relaunch until game over.
nextAction: Change the patrol loop/reset lifecycle so dead patrol teams clear wfbe_patrol_active during a running match; smoke patrol launch, patrol death and relaunch.

summary: The zero-camp fallback in GetTotalCamps helpers affects more than UI counts: it feeds camp-gated capture mode 2, threeway defender respawn and depot infantry gating.
nextAction: Split real-count helpers from safe-denominator helpers or add caller-specific zero-camp guards; smoke capture mode 2, threeway respawn and depot infantry buys on zero-camp towns.

feature: Town/economy, respawn/service/EASA, upstream provenance and loop topology scout harvest
summary: Four mini scouts returned after Spark quota blocked the requested starts. Promoted non-duplicate deltas: stable AIBuyUnit/Server_BuyUnit is latent until caller proof; resource cap suppresses side supply, paychecks and AI commander funds; town/camp capture rewards are client-local mixed authority; respawn penalty mode 5 can still strip gear on unpaid base/HQ respawn; EASA/service stale-context and exact-funds issues are sharper; Community & Dev now carries contributor-density and upstream head-delta provenance; performance pages now warn which visible loops are intentionally throttled/cache-heavy.
nextAction: Use as source-backed docs baseline for future town/economy, respawn/service/EASA, upstream-provenance and performance-loop work.
